result.stdout_text:
Available commands:
  append   — Append to a file from inline content or stdin.
  calc     — Evaluate a safe arithmetic expression.
  cat      — Read text from one or more files, or splice piped stdin with `-`.
  cp       — Copy a file or directory inside the rooted virtual file system.
  diff     — Compare files or directories with normal, unified, or context output.
  echo     — Write arguments back as plain text output. Supports -n, -e, and -E.
  fetch    — Call your configured fetch tool and return text or JSON.
  find     — Recursively list files and directories with optional filters.
  grep     — Filter lines by pattern. Supports a common GNU grep subset.
  head     — Show the first N lines or bytes from stdin or a file.
  help     — List commands or show detailed help for one command.
  json     — Inspect JSON from stdin or a file: pretty, keys, get.
  ls       — List files or directories in the rooted virtual file system.
  memory   — Search, store, or inspect lightweight runtime-scoped working memory.
  mkdir    — Create a directory and any missing parents.
  mv       — Move or rename a file or directory.
  rm       — Delete a file or directory recursively.
  search   — Query your configured search tool and return text results.
  sed      — Stream editor with in-place editing, addresses, and substitutions.
  sort     — Sort lines from stdin or a file.
  stat     — Show one-tool file metadata such as size, type, and modified time.
  tail     — Show the last N lines or bytes from stdin or a file.
  tr       — Translate, delete, and squeeze bytes from stdin.
  uniq     — Collapse adjacent duplicate lines from stdin or a file.
  wc       — Count lines, words, and bytes from stdin or a file.
  write    — Write a file from inline content or stdin.

Use: help <command>
Or run a command with no args to get its usage.
